title: Fort Vijf Sinnen

overview.short: Fort Vijf Sinnen (also Vyf Sinnen, Dutch for "the five senses") was a fortification made by the Dutch East India Company in Nagapattinam, then part of Dutch Coromandel (1610-1798),
overview.description: Fort Vijf Sinnen (also Vyf Sinnen, Dutch for "the five senses") was a fortification made by the Dutch East India Company in Nagapattinam, then part of Dutch Coromandel (1610-1798), now Tamil Nadu. The fortification, also described as a castle, was built to protect the interests of the trading company, which shifted the capital of the Coromandel operation from Pulicat to Nagapattinam in 1690, three years after work began on the fort. The heavily armed fort in the end proved useless in the 1781 Siege of Negapatam, in which the British took the fort. In the Treaty of Paris of 1784 which ended the

topPlaces.name: Soundararajaperumal Temple, Nagapattinam
topPlaces.description: The Soundararajaperumal Temple is a temple dedicated to the Hindu god, Vishnu. It is located in Nagapattinam, a town in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Veezhinathar Kovil, Thiruveezhimizhalai
topPlaces.description: Veezhinathar Temple (வீழிநாதர் கோயில், திருவீழிமிழலை ]) is an ancient temple located in Thiruveezhimizhalai dedicated to Lord Shiva. Thiruveezhimizhalai is a revenue village in Kudavasal Taluka of Thiruvarur District in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Negapatam (1758)
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Nagapatam was an indecisive naval battle on 3 August 1758 during the Seven Years' War. A British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock fought a French squadron under Comte d'Aché off the Carnatic coast of India near Negapatam in the second of three battles fought between the two admirals during the war.

topPlaces.name: Action of 6 July 1746
topPlaces.description: The action of 6 July 1746 was an inconclusive naval engagement between the British and French fleets during the War of the Austrian Succession.

topPlaces.name: Akkaraipettai
topPlaces.description: Akkaraipettai is a fishing village located in Kelvelur taluk Nagapattinam district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The population was 6,672 at the 2011 Census.

topPlaces.name: Nandinadesvarar Temple, Poikainallur
topPlaces.description: Nandinadesvarar Temple is a Siva temple in Poigainallur in Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Nagapattinam taluk
topPlaces.description: Nagapattinam taluk is a taluk of Nagapattinam district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Nagapattinam block
topPlaces.description: Nagapattinam block is a revenue block in the Nagapattinam taluk of Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. There are a total of 27 panchayat villages in this block.

items.note: The Soundararajaperumal Temple is a temple dedicated to the Hindu god, Vishnu. It is located in Nagapattinam, a town in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

Veezhinathar Temple (வீழிநாதர் கோயில், திருவீழிமிழலை ]) is an ancient temple located in Thiruveezhimizhalai dedicated to Lord Shiva. Thiruveezhimizhalai is a revenue village in Kudavasal Taluka of Thiruvarur District in Tamil Nadu, India.

The Battle of Nagapatam was an indecisive naval battle on 3 August 1758 during the Seven Years' War. A British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock fought a French squadron under Comte d'Aché off the Carnatic coast of India near Negapatam in the second of three battles fought between the two admirals during the war.
The action of 6 July 1746 was an inconclusive naval engagement between the British and French fleets during the War of the Austrian Succession.
Akkaraipettai is a fishing village located in Kelvelur taluk Nagapattinam district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The population was 6,672 at the 2011 Census.
Nandinadesvarar Temple is a Siva temple in Poigainallur in Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu, India.
Nagapattinam taluk is a taluk of Nagapattinam district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
Nagapattinam block is a revenue block in the Nagapattinam taluk of Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. There are a total of 27 panchayat villages in this block.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fort Vijf Sinnen?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fort Vijf Sinnen is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fort Vijf Sinnen?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (123 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (125 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fort Vijf Sinnen?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fort Vijf Sinnen at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fort Vijf Sinnen?
faq.a: Stays in Fort Vijf Sinnen start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Fort Vijf Sinnen?
faq.a: The top attractions are Soundararajaperumal Temple, Nagapattinam, Veezhinathar Kovil, Thiruveezhimizhalai, Battle of Negapatam (1758), Action of 6 July 1746, Akkaraipettai.
